Overview.title: Umbrella Archive — Live Overview
Overview.body: This section updates dynamically from the archive dataset. The Resident Evil archive can switch between characters, incidents, equipment, chronology, and tactical profiles without reloading the page.

Characters.title: Operative and Threat Records
Characters.body: Chris, Jill, Leon, Claire, Wesker, and Birkin are stored as archive records. Select a subject and the description changes inside the same panel.

Equipment.title: Emergency Equipment Files
Equipment.body: Weapons are presented as survival equipment. The archive changes the description for the combat knife, handgun, shotgun, SMG, bowgun, and rocket launcher instantly.

Chronology.title: Incident Chronology
Chronology.body: The Resident Evil timeline is displayed by year. When a period is selected, the matching incident description appears inside a dedicated archive panel.

locations.name: Spencer Mansion
locations.description: The Arklay Mountains incident site. Beneath the mansion, Umbrella facilities were used for T-Virus development and biological weapons research.

locations.name: Raccoon City
locations.description: The epicenter of Umbrella's collapse in the United States. The city is destroyed after the T-Virus outbreak and the spread of B.O.W. creatures.

locations.name: R.P.D. Station
locations.description: A former administrative building converted into a police station. It became one of the last resistance points during the Raccoon City outbreak.

locations.name: Baker Estate
locations.description: The Baker family property, corrupted by Eveline's mold. The incident marks the series' return to intense survival horror.

locations.name: Eastern European Village
locations.description: The village from Resident Evil Village combines local cult practices, mold-based experimentation, and four domains controlled by mutant lords.

locations.name: Umbrella Underground Lab
locations.description: A research facility used for virus development and biological weapons. Sites like this contain the strongest evidence of Umbrella's crimes.

images.title: Chris Redfield
images.description: Direct combat profile: strength, team protection, and resistance against B.O.W. threats.

images.title: Jill Valentine
images.description: Survival and tactical problem-solving specialist with a balanced field profile.

images.title: Leon S. Kennedy
images.description: Calm under pressure and linked to both the Raccoon City collapse and the Los Illuminados operation.

images.title: Claire Redfield
images.description: Civilian survivor focused on protecting vulnerable people in extreme biohazard situations.

images.title: Albert Wesker
images.description: Antagonist profile: control, experimentation, manipulation, and extreme biological ambition.

images.title: Matilda Handgun
images.description: A reliable sidearm associated with Raccoon City field survival and precise tactical response.

images.title: Shotgun
images.description: A space-control weapon useful against close and aggressive biohazard threats.

images.title: Rocket Launcher
images.description: The final answer to massive mutations. In the archive, it usually marks the end of a boss encounter.

timeline.title: Resident Evil Series Launch
timeline.description: The first game introduces Spencer Mansion, Umbrella Corporation, and the survival horror formula built around limited resources.

timeline.title: Arklay Incident and Raccoon City Collapse
timeline.description: The key year of the series: S.T.A.R.S. investigates the mansion, and Raccoon City falls to biological contamination.

timeline.title: Los Illuminados Operation
timeline.description: Leon investigates the disappearance of the president's daughter and uncovers the Las Plagas parasite.

timeline.title: Major Confrontation with Wesker
timeline.description: Resident Evil 5 shifts the conflict to a global scale and closes the direct confrontation with Albert Wesker.

timeline.title: Baker Estate Incident
timeline.description: Resident Evil 7 returns to personal horror, first-person perspective, and mold-based contamination.

timeline.title: Eastern European Incident
timeline.description: Resident Evil Village expands the mold storyline through Miranda, the village lords, and the Winters family trauma.

incidentWorld.label: United States
incidentWorld.title: United States — Raccoon City / Arklay
incidentWorld.description: Home to the key Umbrella incidents: Spencer Mansion, Raccoon City, and underground research facilities.

incidentWorld.label: Rural Europe
incidentWorld.title: Europe — Los Illuminados
incidentWorld.description: A rural region controlled by Los Illuminados, where Las Plagas replaces the classic T-Virus threat model.

incidentWorld.label: Africa
incidentWorld.title: Kijuju / Tricell
incidentWorld.description: The Resident Evil 5 incident expands biological experimentation to a global scale and connects Umbrella's legacy to other organizations.

incidentWorld.label: Eastern Europe
incidentWorld.title: Isolated Village
incidentWorld.description: The site of Resident Evil Village, dominated by Miranda and mutations connected to the mold.

raccoonCity.label: R.P.D.
raccoonCity.title: Raccoon Police Department
raccoonCity.description: A resistance point and central location for Leon, Claire, and the city's remaining survivors.

raccoonCity.label: Streets
raccoonCity.title: Raccoon City Streets
raccoonCity.description: Contaminated streets filled with zombies, civil panic, and very limited resources.

raccoonCity.label: Sewers
raccoonCity.title: Underground System
raccoonCity.description: A hidden route connecting the city to Umbrella facilities and Birkin's experimental traces.

raccoonCity.label: NEST
raccoonCity.title: NEST Laboratory
raccoonCity.description: An advanced Umbrella facility where the G-Virus and other projects were documented.

umbrellaComplex.label: Archive
umbrellaComplex.title: Umbrella Archive
umbrellaComplex.description: The file section stores reports about characters, weapons, games, incidents, and experiments.

umbrellaComplex.label: Testing
umbrellaComplex.title: B.O.W. Testing Zone
umbrellaComplex.description: A controlled area used to evaluate subject reactions to biological threats.

umbrellaComplex.label: Quarantine
umbrellaComplex.title: Quarantine Sector
umbrellaComplex.description: The area where unstable profiles and failed assessment answers are isolated for correction.

umbrellaComplex.label: Control
umbrellaComplex.title: Control Room
umbrellaComplex.description: The central node for score checks, field validation, and real-time assessment feedback.

characters.name: Chris Redfield
characters.description: A direct fighter focused on protection and frontal action.

characters.name: Jill Valentine
characters.description: A tactical specialist who remains calm and effective in impossible situations.

characters.name: Leon S. Kennedy
characters.description: An adaptable survivor skilled at improvisation and civilian protection.

characters.name: Claire Redfield
characters.description: An empathetic and resilient profile focused on saving vulnerable people.

characters.name: Albert Wesker
characters.description: Control, manipulation, experimentation, and extreme biological ambition.

characters.name: William Birkin
characters.description: G-Virus researcher associated with uncontrolled mutation and the fall of Raccoon City.

banner.title: Chris Redfield
banner.text: ALPHA profile: direct intervention against B.O.W. threats.

banner.title: Jill Valentine
banner.text: OMEGA profile: survival, strategy, and mental resistance.

banner.title: Leon S. Kennedy
banner.text: DELTA profile: rapid adaptation during urban and rural incidents.

banner.title: Albert Wesker
banner.text: CLASSIFIED profile: extreme risk and unauthorized biological potential.

banner.title: Matilda Handgun
banner.text: STANDARD ISSUE profile: reliable sidearm for controlled survival under biohazard pressure.
